about: Sample seed document: Camp Arifjan, Kuwait — security (Triple Canopy), base operations (V2X),
and vehicle/equipment maintenance (split between Amentum's APS-5 mission under the 401st AFSB
and V2X's LOGCAP V base-fleet support). Built from public sources (defense.gov announcements,
GAO protest decisions, USAspending/HigherGov records, contractor press releases) as of July 2026.
Values are announced amounts or ceilings as noted and some dates are approximate — verify on
USAspending.gov / SAM.gov. All people, phones, emails, ratings, and milestones are FICTIONAL
placeholders. CAGE/UEI left blank — fill from SAM.gov entity registrations.
NOTE: the V2X and Amentum entries share contract numbers with seed-data-kuwait.json, so the
import SKIPS them if those are already loaded. To take the refreshed versions, delete the old
contracts in the UI first, then import this file.

contracts.title: Security Support Services — Camp Arifjan & Camp Buehring (Triple Canopy)
contracts.milestone30: Monthly guard-post staffing and training-certification report to the KO.
contracts.milestone60: Access-control and force-protection equipment surveillance check.
contracts.milestone90: Quarterly performance review with ASG-Kuwait Directorate of Emergency Services.
contracts.milestone120: Option-year exercise decision point; verify DBA insurance and manning ceilings.
contracts.notes: Task order under the CENTCOM Security Support Services (SSS) IDIQ W52P1J-19-D-0005, awarded 22 Jul 2020 by ACC-RI. Announced at $198M with a potential seven-year period; public records show a ceiling up to ~$398.5M with options. Continues Triple Canopy's security presence in Kuwait dating to ~2011. End date shown assumes options exercised — verify current PoP and any follow-on on USAspending.gov/SAM.gov.
contracts.description: Security operations support for DoD personnel and assets at Camp Arifjan and Camp Buehring: police administration, law enforcement support, access control, static and roving guard forces, security force supervision, and support to force-protection technology.

contractors.notes: Prime; Constellis subsidiary. ~9 years incumbent on Kuwait security prior to this task order. Sample rating data.

contracts.title: LOGCAP V — Kuwait Task Order (V2X): Base Ops, Security & Base Fleet Maintenance
contracts.milestone30: Monthly award-fee board inputs; validate motor-pool service-order backlog at Arifjan.
contracts.milestone60: QASP surveillance summary due to KO; NTV fleet readiness metrics review.
contracts.milestone90: Quarterly performance evaluation board with V2X program management.
contracts.milestone120: Extension/option planning with ACC-RI LOGCAP contracting office.
contracts.notes: Task order under LOGCAP V IDIQ W52P1J-19-D-0045; ~$2.2B obligated to date. Army announced 17 Apr 2025 that V2X's Kuwait, Iraq, and INDOPACOM task orders are extended through June 2030. At Camp Arifjan this covers base life support, facilities O&M, transportation, and maintenance of the base operations / non-tactical vehicle fleet (tactical & prepositioned equipment maintenance sits with the APS-5 contract — see W52P1J-12-G-0028).
contracts.description: Logistics Civil Augmentation Program V support services in Kuwait: base operations, security support, transportation, theater logistics, and base-fleet vehicle maintenance at Camp Arifjan, Camp Buehring, Udairi Range, Camp Patriot, KCIA, and Port of Shuaiba.

contractors.notes: Prime. Incumbent lineage from K-BOSSS; merged into V2X in 2022. Sample rating data.

contracts.title: APS-5 Kuwait — Equipment & Vehicle Maintenance, Camp Arifjan (Amentum / AFSBn-Kuwait)
contracts.milestone30: Equipment readiness / inventory accuracy reporting for APS-5 warehouse sets.
contracts.milestone60: COSIS surveillance; spot-check maintenance quality on tracked and wheeled fleets.
contracts.milestone90: Recompete transition planning sync with AFSBn-Kuwait and ACC-RI.
contracts.milestone120: Closeout prep: GFP reconciliation, CPARS narrative drafting.
contracts.notes: The tactical vehicle/equipment maintenance mission at Camp Arifjan (Bradleys, HMMWVs, APCs, and other APS-5 prepositioned stock) — performed by Amentum under this EAGLE BOA order, managed by AFSBn-Kuwait / 401st AFSB. Recompete status: the ~$128.2M APS-5 Kuwait LOGCAP V task order went to Amentum-Parsons JV in Jul 2024, but GAO SUSTAINED V2X's protest (B-422697, Oct 2024) and directed the Army to re-evaluate and make a new award decision; Amentum-Parsons' reconsideration request was denied. Verify the current performer before relying on this entry.
contracts.description: Program management, warehousing, inventory control, and field-level maintenance of Army Prepositioned Stocks-5 equipment — the tactical vehicle and equipment maintenance workload at Camp Arifjan and associated Kuwait sites.

contractors.notes: Prime (formerly AECOM Management Services); incumbent pending APS-5 recompete re-award. Sample rating data.
JV awardee of the 2024 APS-5 Kuwait recompete task order (~$128.2M) — award set aside after GAO sustained V2X's protest; listed here for tracking the transition. Sample rating data.
